text: Complex number decision (v0.3.5 and future tier). v0.3.5 inputs are real; complex appears only in outputs, so NO complex storage/kernel tier this phase. (1) Add final case class Complex(re: Double, im: Double) as a boundary value type — boxed is fine, it never enters kernels or arrays (PRD: materialize Complex only at the API boundary). (2) Nonsymmetric eigen results store SoA (re: DVec, im: DVec) plus a real DMat of eigenvectors packed in LAPACK real-Schur convention (conjugate pair lambda = a+-bi owns two adjacent columns v_re, v_im encoding v_re +- i*v_im); expose via typed ComplexEigenPairs accessors (eigenvalue(i): Complex, isRealPair(i), eigenvector(i): (DVec, DVec)) so the convention is never caller-visible. (3) Symmetric/Hermitian-real APIs stay complex-free: EigenDecomposition carries DVec eigenvalues + real DMat — no Complex in its signature. (4) FUTURE TIER (deferred; trigger = complex-INPUT problems: complex Hermitian eigen, complex solves): CVec/CMat with INTERLEAVED packed storage ([re0, im0, re1, im1, ...] in DoubleArray/Float64Array), matching BLAS/LAPACK zomplex layout so the v0.5 FFM backend passes buffers to zgemm/zgeev with zero conversion. The v0.3.5 SoA result plumbing and the future interleaved storage tier are separate concerns — do NOT unify them; SoA is a result-type convenience over existing DVecs, interleaved is the kernel-tier contract.
